First Annual CGEC Alpha Project Retreat
November 21-23, 2003

Costanoa Coastal Lodge & Camp
2001 Rossi Road at Highway 1
Pescadero, CA 94060

A few dozen scientists gathered on a chilly Fall weekend for the First Annual Center for Genomic Experimentation and Computation (CGEC) Alpha Project Retreat. The location was picture perfect: California coast to the West, rolling hills to the East, sprawling lodge and campground off historic Highway 1, crisp mornings, stunning sunsets, roaring campfires, steaming outdoor hottub, daily chef-prepared meals, extraordinarily accomodating staff. Two and a half days of intense scientific discussions and planning, backdropped by the majesty that is the California coast.

This website was created to capture the moments and to serve as repository of information about the retreat and its participants.

About The Alpha Project

The Alpha Project is an 5-year, interdisciplinary, multi-institutional collaborative research effort to model and predict the behavior of an biological information processing pathway: the yeast pheromone signal transduction pathway. Quantifying how cells sense and respond to various stimuli and creating a model that will accurately predict these responses are the first important steps to understanding diseases and eventually to tailoring precise treatments to each individual.

The Alpha Project is the flagship activity of the Center for Genomic Experimentation and Computation (CGEC). The CGEC was created to pursue interdisciplinary projects that will enable broad understanding of cellular and organismic behavior and to work towards making predictive models of biological systems by combining experimentation and computation. In 2002 the National Human Genome Research Institute (NHGRI) of the NIH designated the CGEC as a Center of Excellence in Genomic Sciences.

The CGEC core is at the Molecular Sciences Institute. Participating members of the Center are located at the following organizations:

Caltech
MIT
Pacific Northwest National Laboratory.
Systems Biology Markup Language

(More information about the Alpha Project)
